reasoning: The historical baseline tool provided a combined probability of 0.57 that the price on June 29 will be above 136.9 based on a seasonal bump often seen for DVA in late June. Over the last month, the stock has traded sideways around 136-138 after dropping in February following news of a Berkshire sell-off and lowered guidance. DaVita also actively repurchased $680M in stock earlier this year, establishing a floor of sorts, and it does not pay a dividend (which removes the risk of scheduled price drops padding the comparison against the June 22 baseline). Because of these factors and the tool's recommendation, I hold a slight positive lean for the short term. Following the explicit guidance that long-term predictions should be closer to 0.5 as uncertainty rises, I start at 0.55 for the immediate resolution date and gradually pull probabilities down to 0.51 for the six-month horizon constraint.
